skill.name: gate-exchange-marketanalysis
skill.description: Gate Exchange market analysis skill for read-only liquidity, momentum, liquidation, funding, basis, manipulation risk, order book, slippage, K-line, and weekend volume analysis using Gate MCP market data.
skill.summary: Analyze Gate Exchange market depth, momentum, derivatives signals, slippage, and liquidity patterns.
skill.author: gate

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are primarily Markdown backticks, MCP tool names, and market-analysis terminology. No prompt injection, executable code, credential handling, local file writes, or trading actions were found. The remaining risk is a low-severity network dependency on Gate MCP read-only market data.

low_findings.title: Networked Market Data Dependency Through Gate MCP
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE with low severity: The skill depends on Gate MCP market-data calls and a Gate website reference. This creates a network dependency, but the documented behavior is read-only and states there are no trading operations or credential storage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files repeatedly specify Gate MCP market-data calls and read-only analysis. The same documentation states no external scripts, no credential handling, and no trading operations.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Matches Are Markdown Tool Names
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The scanner matched backtick-delimited Markdown, fenced examples, and MCP tool names. The reviewed files contain documentation instructions for Gate MCP calls, not Ruby backtick execution or shell invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown documentation, tables, or code fences naming MCP tools. No executable Ruby, shell command, subprocess call, or command interpolation was found in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Matches Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The high-severity weak-cryptography alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code. Reviewed lines describe market analysis, Markdown files, or Gate MCP tool names and contain no MD5, SHA-1, or crypto API use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed content is prose and Markdown for exchange-market analysis. No evidence of weak cryptographic algorithms, hashing, encryption, or credential handling was found.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Matches Are Domain Terms
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static reconnaissance alerts map to market-analysis terms such as order book, depth, volume, and contract data. The reviewed scenario lines request exchange market data, not host, network, or operating-system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines list Gate market-data parameters and required fields. No commands or instructions collect local system identity, files, processes, users, or network configuration.

content.user_title: Analyze Gate Market Liquidity and Risk
content.value_statement: Crypto market checks often require several order book, ticker, funding, and candle data points. This skill routes the request to a Gate MCP workflow and produces a structured read-only market report.
actual_capabilities: Routes user requests across ten Gate Exchange market-analysis scenarios.
Compares order book depth, spreads, 24h volume, and 30-day average volume for liquidity checks.
Analyzes buy and sell pressure using trades, tickers, candlesticks, order book depth, and funding rates.
Reviews liquidation, funding, basis, and premium signals for futures-focused questions.
Simulates market-buy slippage by walking the ask side of spot or futures order books.
Builds K-line support, resistance, breakout, and weekend-versus-weekday liquidity summaries.
limitations: Requires Gate MCP to be installed, configured, and available to the assistant.
Provides analysis only and does not place, modify, or cancel trades.
Depends on available Gate MCP fields, so missing market data can limit report detail.
Outputs are market-data analysis and should not be treated as investment advice.

use_cases.title: Check execution quality before a trade
use_cases.description: Estimate liquidity, spread, and expected slippage before sizing a spot or futures market order.

use_cases.title: Monitor derivatives market stress
use_cases.description: Review liquidation activity, funding rates, basis, and premium signals for contract markets.

use_cases.title: Explain market structure to clients
use_cases.description: Convert order book, volume, and K-line data into clear explanations and structured reports.

prompt_templates.title: Basic liquidity check
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze ETH_USDT liquidity on Gate. Include depth, spread, 24h volume, 30-day average volume, and a short risk note.

prompt_templates.title: Momentum and flow review
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether BTC_USDT has stronger buy or sell pressure over the last 24 hours. Use trades, ticker data, candles, depth, and funding if relevant.

prompt_templates.title: Funding and basis screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Find Gate futures contracts with notable funding or basis signals. Exclude thin books and explain why each candidate matters.

prompt_templates.title: Full execution scenario
prompt_templates.prompt: Simulate a 20000 USDT market buy for ADA_USDT on Gate spot. Walk the ask ladder, estimate slippage, and explain execution risk.

output_examples.input: How is ETH_USDT liquidity on Gate?
output: The report summarizes current order book depth, best bid and ask, spread, 24h volume, and 30-day average volume.
It assigns a liquidity rating and explains whether the pair looks suitable for larger market orders.
It ends with a short note that the analysis is data-based and not investment advice.
The report lists recent K-line context, support levels, resistance levels, 24h change, and volume confirmation.
It classifies the breakout signal as weak, moderate, or strong based on price and volume evidence.

output_examples.input: What slippage would a 10000 USDT ADA_USDT market buy have?
output: The report states the requested pair and quote amount, summarizes estimated average fill price, and compares it with the best ask.
It explains whether the estimated slippage is low, moderate, or high for the current order book.

best_practices: Specify the exact pair and whether the question is for spot or futures.
Provide a quote amount when asking for slippage simulation.
Ask for the assumptions and missing fields when MCP data is incomplete.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to place trades or manage exchange accounts.
Do not treat funding or basis signals as guaranteed arbitrage profit.
Do not rely on stale reports during fast market conditions.

faq.question: Does this skill place trades?
faq.answer: No. The documented behavior is read-only market analysis through Gate MCP.

faq.question: What data source does it use?
faq.answer: It uses Gate MCP tools for order books, tickers, candlesticks, trades, funding rates, liquidation data, and premium indexes.

faq.question: Can it analyze both spot and futures markets?
faq.answer: Yes. It routes to spot by default and uses futures tools when the user asks about perpetuals or contracts.

faq.question: What inputs are required for slippage simulation?
faq.answer: The user must provide both a currency pair and a quote amount. The skill should ask for missing values.

faq.question: Is this investment advice?
faq.answer: No. It produces data-based market analysis and should include a disclaimer.

faq.question: Why is the security risk level low?
faq.answer: The review found no executable code or credential handling, but the skill depends on networked Gate MCP market-data access.
